body_excerpt: {"credits": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-49298", "cvss": {"score": 8.8, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 8.8, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-538", "name": "Insertion of Sensitive Information into Externally-Accessible File or Directory"}], "description": "A bug in Apache Airflow's KubernetesExecutor caused JWT tokens used by worker pods to authenticate against the Execution API to be passed to the worker container as command-line arguments visible in the pod spec. An authenticated UI/API user with Kubernetes read-only access to the cluster (e.g. `pods/get` in the Airflow namespace) could harvest the JWT from `kubectl describe pod` output and then call state-mutating Execution API endpoints — triggering Dag runs, clearing runs, reading or writing Variables / Connections / XComs — as if they were a running task. Affects deployments using the `KubernetesExecutor`. Users are advised to upgrade to `apache-airflow` 3.2.2 or later. This is the airflow-core half of the same vulnerability addressed by [CVE-2026-27173](https://www.cve.org/CVERecord?id=CVE-2026-27173), which shipped the apache-airflow-providers-cncf-kubernetes side of the fix. Deployments that already upgraded `apache-airflow-providers-cncf-kubernetes` to 10.17.0 or later per the CVE-2026-27173 advisory should additionally upgrade `apache-airflow` to 3.2.2 or later to close the core-side surface — the two fixes are complementary, not duplicates.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00489, "percentile": 0.38627}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-5j6p-jrrm-6x94", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-07-08T19:45:01Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-5j6p-jrrm-6x94", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-5j6p-jrrm-6x94"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-49298"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-06-01T09:16:20Z", "published_at": "2026-06-01T09:31:15Z", "references": ["https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-49298", "https://github.com/apache/airflow/pull/60108", "https://lists.apache.org/thread/wo09vrks8189dzsot39rvrx3vnx102tt", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-5j6p-jrrm-6x94"], "repository_advisory_url": null, "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/apache/airflow/pull/", "summary": "Apache Airflow: Execution API JWT leaked via KubernetesExecutor worker command-line args", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-07-08T19:45:02Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-5j6p-jrrm-6x94", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "3.2.2", "package": {"ecosystem": "pip", "name": "apache-airflow-core"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 3.2.2"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

reasons: 資産不一致または判定情報不足

title: Apache Airflow: Execution API JWT leaked via KubernetesExecutor worker command-line args
